title: Paul Thomas Anderson Wins Oscar for Best Director
text: Paul Thomas Anderson won the Academy Award for Best Director at the 98th Oscars for "One Battle After Another." This marks his first Oscar win in any category after three previous nominations. The film earned 13 nominations total, including Best Picture and Best Adapted Screenplay. Anderson's victory followed his win at the Directors Guild of America Awards, which historically predicts Oscar outcomes. During his acceptance speech, Anderson expressed gratitude and acknowledged the difficulty of winning the award. He stated: "You make a guy work hard for one of these" and dedicated the recognition to his late producer and assistant director, Adam Somner, with whom he collaborated for nearly two decades. "One Battle After Another" received 13 Oscar nominations overall, including Best Picture and acting nominations for Leonardo DiCaprio, Benicio del Toro, Sean Penn, and Teyana Taylor.
